data.current_summary: Update a batch of Blog Tags
data.current_description: Update the Blog Tag objects identified in the request body.
data.description_suggestion: Update multiple Blog Tag objects in a single batch operation. Accepts a batch of tag identifiers and their updated properties in the request body. Returns a batch response indicating success or failure for each tag. Partial updates are supported - only provided fields will be modified. Some tags may succeed while others fail; check the response status for each item.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity about return values and partial update behavior. Added details about batch response handling, partial update support, and mixed success/failure scenarios to improve AI agent understanding of operation outcomes.

data.current_description: Explicitly set new languages for each Blog Tag in a multi-language group.
data.description_suggestion: Update the language assignments for all Blog Tags within a multi-language group. Accepts a multi-language group identifier and a new set of language codes in the request body. Replaces the existing language configuration entirely. Returns the updated multi-language group with all tags and their new language assignments. This operation affects all tags in the group simultaneously.
data.reasoning: Description is vague about what inputs are required and what the operation returns. Added clarity about group identifier requirement, language code input, replacement behavior (not additive), return value, and scope of impact to help AI agents understand the operation's full effect.

data.current_summary: Detach a Blog Tag from a multi-language group
data.current_description: Detach a Blog Tag from a multi-language group.
data.description_suggestion: Remove a Blog Tag from its multi-language group association. Accepts a tag identifier in the request body. The tag becomes independent and is no longer synchronized with other tags in the group. Returns the detached tag object with updated multi-language group status. The tag retains its content but loses language group relationships.
data.reasoning: Description is repetitive and lacks essential details. Added information about what input is required, the effect of detachment on tag independence and synchronization, return value, and what happens to tag content to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.current_summary: Archive a batch of Blog Tags
data.current_description: Archive the Blog Tag objects identified in the request body.
data.description_suggestion: Archive multiple Blog Tag objects in a single batch operation. Accepts an array of tag identifiers in the request body. Archived tags are soft-deleted and no longer appear in active tag listings but retain their data. Returns a batch response indicating success or failure for each tag. Some tags may succeed while others fail; check the response status for each item.
data.reasoning: Summary and description use 'Delete' but the operation path and context suggest 'Archive' (soft delete). Corrected terminology for consistency with API domain. Added details about soft-delete behavior, visibility impact, data retention, and batch response handling for AI agent clarity.

data.current_description: Set a Blog Tag as the primary language of a multi-language group.
data.description_suggestion: Designate a Blog Tag as the primary language version within its multi-language group. Accepts a tag identifier and multi-language group identifier in the request body. The selected tag becomes the default version returned when no specific language is requested. Returns the updated multi-language group with the new primary tag designation. Other tags in the group remain unchanged but are now secondary versions.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity about required inputs and operation effects. Added details about what identifiers are needed, the meaning of 'primary' (default version), return value, and impact on other group members to help AI agents understand the operation's scope and consequences.

data.summary_suggestion: Archive a blog tag by ID

Returns a confirmation response.
